sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. stock rises Tuesday, still underperforms market
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) stock rose 0.51% on Tuesday, closing at $92.77, despite an overall strong day for the stock market where the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw gains. The stock remained $5.21 below its 52-week high and underperformed against competitors like NVIDIA Corp., Intel Corp., and Texas Instruments Inc. Trading volume was lower than its 50-day average.

feed.title: AMD Promotes Several Executives, Outlines Major Growth Areas
feed.summary: AMD has promoted five executives, including three to Senior Vice President roles in CPU, GPU, and product technology architecture, and two to Executive Vice President roles in sales and finance. These promotions, which also include significant pay raises for some, aim to support the company's long-term growth and ensure continued momentum in strategically important projects like Zen 4/5, RDNA3/CDNA2, and Xilinx integration. AMD stated that aligning and expanding its senior leadership team around high-priority growth opportunities is crucial for its trajectory.

feed.title: Rock star chip designer Jim Keller joins Tenstorrent
feed.summary: Legendary computer chip designer Jim Keller has joined Toronto startup Tenstorrent Inc. as its new president and chief technology officer. Keller, known for his work at AMD, Apple, and Tesla, will guide Tenstorrent in its mission to create a machine learning training chip that utilizes software to optimize computing power and increase efficiency, challenging established players like Intel, AMD, and Nvidia.

feed.title: Cushman & Wakefield Finalize Sale of Austin Office Property
feed.summary: Cushman & Wakefield facilitated the sale of 7000 West at Lantana, a 136,000-square-foot office property in Austin, fully leased to Smith + Nephew and Advanced Micro Devices Inc. The sale, whose price was not disclosed, highlights a growing trend of tech companies relocating to Texas, with major moves by Hewlett Packard Enterprise and Oracle also noted. This transaction reflects the strong demand for specialized office/lab products in the Austin market.

feed.title: 15 Biggest Semiconductor Companies in the World
feed.summary: This article lists and ranks the 15 largest semiconductor companies globally based on revenue, market cap, assets, and employee count. It highlights the critical role of semiconductors in modern technology, discusses the industry's economic impact, and outlines individual company profiles. The article also touches on geopolitical factors affecting the semiconductor market.

feed.title: 25 of the Best Stocks to Own in January
feed.summary: This article identifies Huntington Ingalls Industries Inc (NASDAQ:HII) as a top stock for January, highlighting its historical performance of averaging a 5.7% gain in January over the past decade with 100% positive returns. It suggests that now is an opportune time to consider buying options on HII due to relatively low volatility expectations and potential for a rally as investor pessimism might unwind. The stock's current price is $170.47.

feed.title: Intel Talks With TSMC, Samsung to Outsource Some Chip Production
feed.summary: Intel Corp. is in discussions with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) and Samsung Electronics Co. to potentially outsource the production of some of its advanced chips. This comes after repeated delays in Intel's own manufacturing processes. While Intel still hopes for improvements in its in-house production, any outsourced components from TSMC would not be available until 2023 at the earliest and would utilize existing manufacturing processes.

feed.title: AI vision processor enables 8K video at 30fps in under 2W
feed.summary: Ambarella has launched its CV5 AI vision processor, a 5nm SoC that delivers 8K video encoding at 30fps with under 2 Watts of power consumption. This highly power-efficient chip is designed for intelligent camera systems in automotive, consumer (including drones), and robotic applications. It combines Ambarella’s CVflow AI engine with dual Arm A76 CPUs and supports up to 14 cameras, making it suitable for a wide range of AI-based algorithms and high-resolution video capture.

feed.title: The 12 Coolest New Laptops At CES 2021
feed.summary: This article highlights 12 of the most notable new laptops introduced at CES 2021, focusing on innovations for the work-from-home environment. Key trends include 5G connectivity, 11th-gen Intel processors, enhanced collaboration features like improved audio, and refined designs with better portability and reduced blue light displays. The featured laptops come from major brands such as Lenovo, Dell, HP, Acer, Samsung, and Dynabook.

feed.title: Samsung Surges After Intel Is Said to Consider Chip Outsourcing
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics Co. saw a significant stock increase following reports that Intel Corp. is considering outsourcing the production of some of its advanced chips to Samsung and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC). This potential move represents a major strategic shift for Intel, which has experienced delays in its own chip fabrication processes. While a final decision is pending, any outsourced components from TSMC are not expected to market until 2023 at the earliest.

feed.title: AMD Announces World’s Best Mobile Processors¹ In CES 2021 Keynote
feed.summary: AMD unveiled its Ryzen 5000 Series Mobile Processors at CES 2021, featuring the "Zen 3" core architecture for laptops, promising enhanced performance and battery life for gamers, creators, and professionals. The company also announced Ryzen PRO 5000 Series Mobile Processors for commercial users and made Ryzen Threadripper PRO Processors available directly to consumers. Over 150 consumer and commercial notebooks are expected to launch in 2021 featuring these new Ryzen 5000 Series Mobile Processors.

feed.title: CES 2021: HP’s 10 Coolest New Laptops And Convertibles
feed.summary: HP announced a new lineup of laptops and convertibles at CES 2021, featuring updates to its ProBook and EliteBook series. These new devices emphasize portability and performance, with models powered by either AMD Ryzen or Intel Core processors. Key advancements include enhanced security features, improved collaboration tools, optional 5G connectivity, and integrated Tile tracking in some premium models.

feed.title: NVIDIA Ampere Architecture Powers Record 70+ New GeForce RTX Laptops
feed.summary: NVIDIA has launched over 70 new GeForce RTX 30 Series laptops powered by the NVIDIA Ampere architecture, offering up to 2x efficiency and featuring third-generation Max-Q technology. These new laptops deliver stunning ray-traced gaming experiences and enhance content creation, with models starting at $999. The lineup includes RTX 3060, RTX 3070, and RTX 3080 GPUs, bringing significant performance improvements over previous generations and introducing advanced features like Dynamic Boost 2.0, WhisperMode 2.0, Resizable BAR, and NVIDIA DLSS.

feed.title: NVIDIA Ampere Architecture for Every Gamer: GeForce RTX 3060 Available Late February, At $329
feed.summary: NVIDIA is launching the GeForce RTX 3060 in late February for $329, bringing its Ampere architecture to a wider range of gamers. The new GPU offers significant performance improvements over the GeForce GTX 1060, including twice the raster performance and 10x the ray tracing performance, along with 12GB of GDDR6 memory. It also supports key advancements like NVIDIA DLSS, Resizable BAR, and NVIDIA Reflex, improving gaming, content creation, and streaming experiences.

feed.title: Supermicro Unveils Threadripper PRO Powered Workstation with Double-Width GPU Support
feed.summary: Supermicro has unveiled a new server-grade, high-end workstation (AS-5014A-TT) powered by the AMD Ryzen Threadripper PRO Processor, supporting up to 64 cores and 128 PCI-E 4.0 lanes. This workstation is designed for demanding professional workloads such as media content creation, scientific research, and AI, offering support for up to four double-width GPUs and 2 TB of DDR4 3200 memory. Supermicro emphasizes its advanced design and architecture, aiming to significantly boost productivity for professionals.

feed.title: Intel CEO Bob Swan to be replaced with VMWare's Pat Gelsinger
feed.summary: Intel CEO Bob Swan is stepping down in February 2021 and will be replaced by VMWare CEO Pat Gelsinger. Swan's departure follows a challenging period for Intel, marked by delays in producing its advanced 7-nanometer chips and increased competition. Gelsinger, a former Intel CTO, is expected to refocus the company on its engineering culture and execution.

feed.title: Intel, Under Pressure to Rethink Its Business, Ousts Its Chief Executive (Published 2021)
feed.summary: Intel has ousted its chief executive, Robert Swan, amid pressure from an activist investor and struggles with manufacturing problems that have cost the company its technology lead. Patrick Gelsinger, a former Intel veteran and CEO of VMware, will replace Swan next month. This leadership change aims to bring an engineer back to the top role to address the company's technical issues and stagnant stock price.

feed.title: In return to Intel, VMware chief Pat Gelsinger to take over as CEO
feed.summary: Intel Corp. has appointed VMware CEO Pat Gelsinger as its new Chief Executive, replacing Robert Swan. Gelsinger, a former Intel CTO, is expected to lead the company's turnaround amidst investor pressure and challenges in chip manufacturing, with his appointment effective February 15. The news caused Intel's shares to rise, while VMware's shares fell due to Gelsinger's departure.

feed.title: Qualcomm scoops up Apple vets' Santa Clara chip startup Nuvia for $1.4B
feed.summary: Qualcomm has acquired Santa Clara-based chip startup Nuvia, founded by former Apple veterans John Bruno, Gerard Williams III, and Manu Gulati, for $1.4 billion. This acquisition price represents a 159% premium over Nuvia's valuation during its September funding round. The deal highlights Qualcomm's strategic move in chip technology.

feed.title: Qualcomm to acquire NUVIA for $1.4 billion – A major shift for the CPU market
feed.summary: Qualcomm has announced its acquisition of NUVIA for $1.4 billion, a move that will allow Qualcomm to re-enter the custom CPU market with a highly experienced design team. NUVIA was founded by veterans from companies like Apple and AMD and aims to develop high-performance ARM CPU cores for various devices, including laptops and servers, potentially challenging Intel and AMD. This acquisition signals a significant strategic shift for Qualcomm, though the impact of NUVIA's technology on consumer products is still years away.

feed.title: Intel CEO Bob Swan Is Replaced by VMware’s Pat Gelsinger
feed.summary: Intel CEO Bob Swan is being replaced by VMware CEO Pat Gelsinger, who is returning to Intel to address the company's challenges in the chip industry. Gelsinger's appointment is seen as a move to bring back technical leadership and address manufacturing delays that have plagued Intel, causing it to lag behind competitors. The change in leadership was welcomed by investors and analysts who believe Gelsinger is the best option to lead Intel through its critical transformation period.

feed.title: HP EliteBook x360 1030 G7 review: A top-quality 13-inch 2-in-1 ultraportable
feed.summary: The HP EliteBook x360 1030 G7 is a 13.3-inch 2-in-1 ultraportable laptop praised for its excellent build quality, all-day battery life, and useful features like HP's Sure View screen privacy and proximity-based security. Despite using 10th-generation Intel processors and lacking discrete graphics, the laptop offers a premium experience with a comfortable keyboard and a bright display. It is recommended for users seeking a feature-rich, robust, and portable convertible laptop for business use.

feed.title: TSMC to Spend Up to Record $28 Billion on Advanced Chips, Capacity
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC), a major chip supplier, announced plans to significantly increase its capital expenditures to between $25 billion and $28 billion in 2021. This substantial investment, representing at least a 47% year-over-year increase, aims to develop advanced chips and expand plant capacity. The move comes in response to a global surge in semiconductor demand that has resulted in supply shortages.

feed.title: Intel reportedly plans to switch to TSMC to make 7nm GPUs
feed.summary: Intel is reportedly planning to outsource the manufacturing of its 7nm GPUs to TSMC, due to internal struggles with 7nm chip development. While Intel has historically manufactured its own processors, this move signifies a shift towards outsourcing to keep pace with rivals like AMD and Nvidia, who already use TSMC for their 7nm products. Despite this, Intel is expected to continue manufacturing its 10nm CPUs in-house for the time being.

feed.title: Top Analyst Upgrades and Downgrades: AMD, Adobe, Chevron, Chipotle, DuPont, Intel, Penn National Gaming and More
feed.summary: This article details top analyst upgrades, downgrades, and initiations announced on January 14, 2021, for various major companies. It provides specific ratings, target prices, and consensus figures for stocks like AMD, Adobe, Chevron, Chipotle, DuPont, and Intel. The report also notes the market context of the announcements, including impending Q4 earnings reports and Intel's CEO change.

feed.title: Intel CEO Bob Swan to Be Replaced by VMware Chief
feed.summary: Intel Corp. Chief Executive Officer Bob Swan will be replaced by VMware Inc. CEO Pat Gelsinger, who is returning to Intel more than a decade after leaving. This leadership change comes as Intel faces manufacturing delays, increased competition, and customers designing their own chips. Gelsinger's appointment is seen as a move to bring back technical and home-grown leadership to address the company's challenges.

feed.title: Qualcomm expands chip lineup with Snapdragon 870 mobile processor
feed.summary: Qualcomm has introduced the Snapdragon 870, a new mobile processor that serves as an upgraded version of its 2020 flagship Snapdragon 865 chip. While not aimed at top-tier flagships, the Snapdragon 870 targets devices priced under $800, offering a faster CPU with clock speeds up to 3.2 gigahertz and supporting 5G and Wi-Fi 6. This strategic launch expands Qualcomm's market coverage in the midrange phone segment, simplifying product development for handset makers.

feed.title: Qualcomm Announces Boosted Snapdragon 870 5G Mobile Platform
feed.summary: Qualcomm Technologies has unveiled the Snapdragon 870 5G Mobile Platform, an enhanced version of the Snapdragon 865 Plus, featuring a Kryo 585 CPU prime core clock speed of up to 3.2 GHz. This new platform aims to deliver increased performance for gaming, global 5G connectivity, and AI experiences. Key customers like Motorola, iQOO, OnePlus, OPPO, and Xiaomi are expected to launch flagship devices powered by the Snapdragon 870 in the first quarter of 2021.

feed.title: Supermicro Unveils NVIDIA GPU Server Test Drive Program with Leading Channel Partners to Deliver Workload Qualification on Remote Supermicro Servers
feed.summary: Supermicro has launched a new GPU test-drive program called STEP, in collaboration with NVIDIA and leading channel partners. This program allows customers to remotely test Supermicro's 2U HGX A100 4-GPU or 4U HGX A100 8-GPU systems, enabling them to qualify their advanced workloads on high-performance platforms. The initiative aims to accelerate time-to-market solutions for AI and HPC applications by providing access to cutting-edge NVIDIA HGX A100 technology.

feed.title: Chinese hacking group ‘Chimera’ targets Taiwanese chip and airline companies
feed.summary: A Chinese advanced threat protection group named "Chimera" is reportedly targeting Taiwanese semiconductor companies to steal intelligence such as IC chip documents, SDKs, IC designs, and source code. Additionally, the group is accused of stealing passenger data from the airline industry, abusing cloud services from Google and Microsoft. Experts suggest these activities, whether state-sponsored or for competitive advantage, highlight a significant cybersecurity threat requiring robust government action.

feed.title: As Intel delivers strong earnings, incoming CEO Pat Gelsinger says chip process on track
feed.summary: Incoming Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger reassured investors that the company's seven-nanometer chip manufacturing technology is on schedule for 2023 chip production, although increasing reliance on external foundries for some products is likely. This announcement came as Intel reported strong fourth-quarter earnings that exceeded analyst expectations, driven by a surge in PC sales. Despite the positive financial results and promises of manufacturing progress, Intel's stock saw a slight decline in after-hours trading.

feed.title: Nvidia Puts Certified AI Servers At Forefront In New Program
feed.summary: Nvidia has launched a new "Nvidia-Certified Systems" program to ensure optimized performance for AI applications on select GPU servers. The initiative aims to guide customers and partners toward certified systems from various OEMs and ODMs, providing performance guarantees and enterprise support. This program is expected to simplify AI infrastructure deployment for businesses and allow partners to focus on value-added AI application development.

feed.title: Micron's 1-alpha process node offers impressive improvements in DRAM density and performance
feed.summary: Micron has begun manufacturing DRAM using its new 1α (1-alpha) process, which offers a 40 percent improvement in memory density and a 15 percent drop in power consumption compared to its 1Z nm technology. This new node supports densities from 8Gb to 16Gb and is being used for DDR4 and LPDDR4 RAM, with plans for broader integration. The company anticipates this will lead to decreased manufacturing costs for DRAM, subsequently resulting in better prices for consumers, although the long-term strategy for scaling DRAM with or without EUV technology remains to be seen against competitors.

feed.title: Intel Appoints Sunil Shenoy as Senior Vice President of Design Engineering Group
feed.summary: Intel Corporation has announced the appointment of Sunil Shenoy as senior vice president and general manager of the Design Engineering Group, effective February 1. Shenoy, a 33-year Intel veteran who previously left in 2014, returns to lead design, development, validation, and manufacturing of IPs and SoCs for client and data center applications. He will report to incoming CEO Pat Gelsinger after February 15, bringing extensive experience in microprocessor and SoC design to strengthen Intel's technical leadership.

feed.title: The Lay Of The FPGA Financial Land
feed.summary: This article analyzes the financial performance of major FPGA makers, Xilinx, Intel's Programmable Solutions Group (Altera), and Achronix. It highlights Xilinx's strong sales and cash position amidst its acquisition by AMD, contrasts it with Intel's FPGA business, and points out the unique financial strategy and profitability of the smaller player Achronix as it goes public via SPAC. The piece underscores the growing importance of FPGAs in compute and networking landscapes.

feed.title: Cover Story: Tech mania amid global chip shortage
feed.summary: The article discusses the tech stock rally fueled by a global chip shortage, benefiting Malaysian semiconductor companies amidst increased demand from consumer electronics and a recovering automotive industry. It highlights differing opinions on whether these tech stocks are overvalued, comparing their valuations to regional and global counterparts, and notes that local institutional investors are drawn to the sector due to limited alternatives.

feed.title: Microchip Announces the World’s First PCI Express® 5.0 Switches
feed.summary: Microchip Technology Inc. announced the world's first PCI Express (PCIe) 5.0 switch solutions, the Switchtec PFX PCIe 5.0 family, to meet the increasing demand for high-performance computing in applications like data analytics and autonomous driving. These switches double interconnect performance and, combined with XpressConnect retimers, make Microchip the sole provider of both PCIe Gen 5 switches and retimer products, offering a complete infrastructure solution. The Switchtec PFX family supports high-density, high-reliability applications and includes advanced debug and diagnostic features to accelerate time-to-market.

feed.title: Microchip Announces World's First PCI Express 5.0 Switches
feed.summary: Microchip Technology Inc. has announced the world's first PCI Express (PCIe) 5.0 switch solutions, the Switchtec PFX PCIe 5.0 family, which doubles interconnect performance for high-demand applications like data analytics and machine learning. These switches, along with Microchip's XpressConnect retimers, provide a comprehensive PCIe Gen 5 infrastructure portfolio. The company states that this innovation will support next-generation machine learning platforms and strengthen the ecosystem for PCIe 5.0 and CXL-enabled solutions, as affirmed by Intel.

feed.title: New SparX-5i family of Ethernet switches from Microchip
feed.summary: Microchip Technology Inc. has announced its SparX-5i family of Ethernet switches, a single-chip, IEEE standards-based solution offering the industry’s most complete Time Sensitive Networking (TSN) feature set. These switches aim to simplify the pathway to TSN compatible infrastructure for industrial automation networks by enabling increased connectivity and network optimization while lowering costs. The SparX-5i family supports various key TSN IEEE standards for real-time communication and offers flexible connectivity with multiple GbE interfaces.

feed.title: Semiconductor Chemical Vapor Deposition (CVD) Equipment
feed.summary: The report provides a strategic analysis of the Global Semiconductor Chemical Vapor Deposition (CVD) Equipment Market and the growth estimates for the forecast period 2021 to 2026. This market is anticipated to witness a CAGR of 8.5% over the forecast period, driven by an increase in demand for microelectronics and consumer electronics. Key players include AIXTRON SE, Applied Materials, Inc., ASM International, and CVD Equipment Corporation.

feed.title: Qualcomm’s Upgradable New Snapdragon X65 Modem Promises to Bring 10gbs speeds to 5G phones as Early as this Year
feed.summary: Qualcomm has announced its new Snapdragon X65 modem-RF system, capable of delivering 10 gigabits per second download speeds to 5G phones as early as this year. The X65 features an upgradable architecture for future-proofing devices, enhanced power efficiency, and incorporates AI to optimize antenna tuning for reliable mmWave connectivity. This move challenges the 5G ecosystem to accelerate technology upgrades and mmWave deployments.

feed.title: Qualcomm objects to Nvidia’s $40 billion Arm acquisition
feed.summary: Qualcomm has formally expressed its opposition to Nvidia's proposed $40 billion acquisition of Arm to regulators worldwide, including the FTC, European Commission, and UK's Competition and Markets Authority. The U.S. chipmaker fears Nvidia could become a gatekeeper of Arm's technology, restricting access for other chipmakers. Regulators are now in a "second phase" of investigation, seeking more information from the involved companies.

feed.title: With new CEO Pat Gelsinger, Intel looks to its past in hopes of securing the future
feed.summary: Pat Gelsinger, a veteran engineer, returns as Intel's CEO to revitalize its manufacturing and engineering, a move seen as a risky but essential gamble to restore the company's pole position in semiconductor production. Despite investor skepticism and past manufacturing failures that allowed rivals to leap ahead, Gelsinger is committed to internal production rather than outsourcing, aiming to bring Intel back to its former glory by drawing on its historical strengths and leadership principles, especially those of former CEO Andy Grove. This strategy is an attempt to address Intel's existential crisis stemming from production delays and increased competition from companies like Apple and AMD.

feed.title: How Does Apple’s M1 Chip Compare to Current Intel and AMD Chips?
feed.summary: This article compares Apple's M1 chip to current Intel and AMD chips, highlighting the M1's Arm-based architecture, 5nm manufacturing process, unified RAM, and superior performance per watt. It discusses benchmark tests showing the M1's strong performance in 3D rendering and some single-threaded tasks, while also noting Intel's counter-claims and the M1's current limitations in gaming. The article concludes by suggesting that while the M1 is a potent threat to x86 dominance, early adopters might face challenges as software developers adapt.

feed.title: Since Three-Phase Motors Mean Triple the Components, TI Banks on Integration
feed.summary: Texas Instruments has released a highly-integrated 48V motor driver, the DRV3255-Q1, designed to reduce PCB area by up to 30% in mild-hybrid electric vehicles (MHEVs). This single IC integrates components typically found in three separate driver circuits for three-phase motors, aiming to lower BOM, cost, and improve signal integrity. The innovation seeks to accelerate MHEV adoption by making them more affordable, safer, and greener while addressing the increasing demand for electrification.

feed.title: Biden Will Order Supply-Chain Review. Chip Stocks Are Surging.
feed.summary: President Joe Biden is expected to sign an executive order to review supply chains in critical industries, including semiconductors. This announcement comes amid a global chip shortage impacting various products from video game consoles to smartphones. Following this news, chip stocks are experiencing a surge.

feed.title: Nvidia sales surge as it struggles to keep gaming chips in stock
feed.summary: Nvidia Corp forecasts better-than-expected fiscal first-quarter revenue, with gaming chips remaining in tight supply due to high global demand and cryptocurrency mining. To counter this, Nvidia is introducing special mining chips and software to discourage miners from using gaming GPUs. The company's aggressive push into AI chips has also contributed to its status as the most valuable semiconductor maker.

feed.title: Drought hits Taiwan chip supply as Biden asks for more
feed.summary: A drought in Taiwan is threatening the global supply of semiconductor chips, a critical component for various industries, including car manufacturing. This crisis comes as President Biden is pushing for increased domestic chip production and has thanked Taiwan for its efforts to address the shortage. The water scarcity is forcing major Taiwanese chip companies like TSMC to resort to using lorries for water supply to maintain production.

feed.title: Does AMD Smart Access Memory work with Ryzen 3000 processors?
feed.summary: AMD Smart Access Memory (SAM) is a feature exclusive to AMD Ryzen 5000 series CPUs when paired with Radeon RX 6000 series GPUs and AMD 500 series chipsets. It does not work with older Ryzen 3000 processors. SAM leverages a fully open data channel between the CPU and GPU, offering a performance boost in games, although the extent of this boost can vary.

feed.title: Cornerstone Commentary to Feature Former Sega of America CEO
feed.summary: The University of Kentucky and Gen.G are continuing their monthly esports speaker series, Cornerstone Commentary, with former Sega of America CEO, Tom Kalinske. Kalinske will discuss his experiences at Mattel, the Sega vs. Nintendo rivalry, and his post-console war endeavors, including his current role as executive chairman of Global Education Learning. The series aims to provide students with insights from leaders in the esports and video game industry and build connections within Kentucky's esports community.

feed.title: AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT GPU appears in leaked images
feed.summary: Images of what appear to be the ASUS Radeon RX 6700 XT GPU, specifically the DUAL and TUF models, have leaked online ahead of its anticipated announcement on March 3, 2021. The RX 6700 XT is expected to be an RDNA 2-based successor to the RX 5700 XT, featuring a Navi 22 GPU with 40 CUs, 2,560 Stream Processors, and 12GB of GDDR6 RAM. Leaked details include specific design elements like slot width, length, and power connectors for both the dual-fan and triple-fan (TUF Gaming OC) variants.

feed.title: You can now buy AMD's Ryzen Threadripper Pro CPUs on their own
feed.summary: AMD has made its Ryzen Threadripper Pro CPUs available for individual purchase. Previously, these powerful processors, designed for demanding professional workloads in workstations, were only available pre-installed in systems like the Lenovo ThinkStation P620. While offering up to 64 cores and extensive PCIe 4.0 lanes, these high-end CPUs come with premium price tags, starting from $1,149 up to $5,489.

feed.title: Barstool's Portnoy backs new BUZZ ETF
feed.summary: Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy announced his backing of the new BUZZ ETF, which is set to debut on the NYSE. This ETF leverages artificial intelligence to analyze social media chatter and track the top 75 large-cap stocks with market capitalizations over $5 billion. Portnoy, who holds an ownership stake in BUZZ, highlighted that the algorithm outperformed the S&P 500 by 40% in 2020.

feed.title: Changing Of The Guard For HPC And Big Iron At HPE
feed.summary: Pete Ungaro, the long-time head of Cray and then HPE's High Performance Computing & Mission Critical Solutions group, is stepping down, with Justin Hotard taking over. This leadership change occurs as HPE continues to navigate a challenging market, focusing on profitability and leveraging the Cray acquisition to scale its HPC business. The company is making strategic bets on services and utility-style pricing for on-premises systems, with recent financial results indicating these efforts are beginning to pay off despite overall revenue and profit declines in some segments.

feed.title: AMD Unveils AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT Graphics Card, Delivering Exceptional 1440p PC Gaming Experiences
feed.summary: AMD has introduced the Radeon RX 6700 XT graphics card, designed to deliver exceptional 1440p PC gaming experiences. Featuring the RDNA 2 architecture, 96MB of Infinity Cache, and 12GB of GDDR6 memory, it offers up to 2X higher performance in select titles compared to older-generation cards. The card also supports advanced features like AMD Smart Access Memory, FidelityFX, and DirectX Raytracing, with an expected starting price of $479.

feed.title: STMicroelectronics Launches Computer-Vision Launchpad for STM32 MCUs
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics has released a new computer-vision launchpad, including an AI firmware function pack (FP-AI-VISION1) and camera-module hardware, designed for embedded developers using STM32 microcontrollers. This solution enables the creation of affordable, powerful edge-based computer-vision applications by running convolutional neural networks locally. The bundle facilitates image classification, human-presence detection, and offers flexibility for developers to retrain neural networks with custom datasets.

feed.title: AMD and Xilinx Set to Merge Before the End of 2021, Looks Promising
feed.summary: Rival chipmakers Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Xilinx are proceeding with a $35 billion all-stock merger, expected to finalize by the end of 2021. The deal will see AMD shareholders owning 74% of the new entity and Xilinx shareholders 26%, with Xilinx stockholders receiving 1.734 shares of AMD for each of their shares. This merger aims to diversify AMD's product portfolio and strengthen its position in computer processors, particularly in data centers and wireless networks.

feed.title: Nvidia vs. AMD: Compare GPU offerings
feed.summary: This article compares the GPU offerings from Nvidia and AMD, focusing on their use in large data centers for tasks like machine learning and high-performance computing. It delves into the specifications of Nvidia's A100 and AMD's Instinct MI100 GPUs, including memory, processing units, and software ecosystems like CUDA and ROCm. The article concludes by noting Nvidia's lead in AI acceleration, AMD's strong competition in HPC, and the impending entry of Intel into the GPU market.

feed.title: NXP could lose $100 million due to weather shutdown of Austin plants
feed.summary: NXP Semiconductors estimates a $100 million revenue loss in the second quarter due to the shutdown of its Austin chip-making plants during last month's winter storms. The company's two fabrication facilities are now resuming initial operations after being non-operational for weeks due to power outages and damage. Samsung's Austin fab also remains non-operational, facing potentially larger financial losses.

feed.title: Energy efficient and scalable: NXP’s new EdgeVerse chips are aimed at industrial edge uses
feed.summary: NXP Semiconductors expanded its EdgeVerse portfolio with new i.MX 8ULP, i.MX 8ULP-CS (cloud secured) with Microsoft Azure Sphere, and i.MX 9 series processors. These new chips feature the EdgeLock secure enclave for enhanced security and Energy Flex architecture for maximized energy efficiency, targeting industrial edge applications. The i.MX 9 series also integrates hardware neural processing units for accelerating machine learning at the edge.

feed.title: AMD Unveils EPYC 'Milan' 7003 CPUs, Zen 3 Comes to 64-Core Server Chips
feed.summary: AMD has officially launched its EPYC 'Milan' 7003 processors, bringing the Zen 3 architecture to the server market with its flagship 64-core EPYC 7763, claiming it to be the world's fastest server processor. These new 7nm chips are drop-in compatible with existing servers and offer significant performance improvements over Intel's Xeon Cascade Lake Refresh chips, particularly in HPC, Cloud, and enterprise workloads, alongside a better price-to-performance ratio. The Milan processors also focus on expanding AMD's hardware and software ecosystems to better cater to a broader range of enterprise customers.

feed.title: AMD EPYC™ 7003 Series CPUs Set New Standard as Highest Performance Server Processor
feed.summary: AMD has announced its new EPYC™ 7003 Series CPUs, including the EPYC 7763, which it claims is the world's highest-performing server processor. These processors leverage "Zen 3" cores to offer significant performance improvements across HPC, cloud, and enterprise workloads, alongside enhanced security features like Secure Encrypted Virtualization-Secure Nested Paging (SEV-SNP). The ecosystem for AMD EPYC processors is expected to expand considerably by the end of 2021 with new cloud instances and OEM platforms from major partners like AWS, Cisco, Dell, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.

feed.title: Samsung to ramp up EUV scanners to take on foundry leader TSMC
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics is significantly increasing its procurement of extreme ultraviolet (EUV) scanners, a critical technology for advanced chip manufacturing, to strengthen its competitive position against foundry market leader TSMC. With ASML being the sole producer of these expensive and limited-supply machines, Samsung's Vice Chairman Jay Y. Lee visited ASML in late 2020 to secure orders for approximately 20 new scanners, adding to its existing 25 units. This aggressive investment aligns with Samsung's "Vision 2030" goal to become the world's top foundry player, although it faces an uphill battle against TSMC's larger market share and investment in R&D.

feed.title: Cisco and AMD Help Businesses Improve Performance, Security and Hybrid Cloud Operations
feed.summary: Cisco and AMD are expanding their collaboration to enhance performance, security, and hybrid cloud operations for businesses. Cisco is introducing new UCS rack server models, powered by AMD EPYC™ 7003 Processors, optimized for high core count and I/O-intensive workloads. This partnership also delivers an industry-leading end-to-end solution for High Frequency Trading by combining Cisco's Nexus Ultra Low Latency networking with the new servers.

feed.title: AMD Raises Bar Against Intel With 3rd-Gen EPYC Milan CPUS
feed.summary: AMD has launched its third-generation EPYC processors, codenamed Milan, aiming to surpass Intel in performance and total cost of ownership for data centers. The new processors offer significant performance improvements, enhanced security features, and backward compatibility with existing server platforms, providing a competitive edge over Intel's upcoming Xeon Scalable chips. AMD emphasizes the value proposition of Milan through its high core density, strong price-performance ratio, and reduced TCO for cloud, enterprise, and HPC workloads.

feed.title: Semiconductors as a shield for Taiwan?
feed.summary: Taiwan's independence is significantly bolstered by its semiconductor industry, particularly Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC). TSMC's critical role in the global supply chain for microprocessors provides Taiwan with a strategic advantage, deterring China from invasion due to potential economic repercussions and compelling the United States to protect its access to these vital technologies. The company's capabilities and importance make a military conflict over Taiwan highly costly for both China and the US, thus acting as a powerful "silicon shield."
feed.source: The Strategist | ASPI's analysis and commentary site
feed.source_domain: The Strategist | ASPI's analysis and commentary site

feed.title: Qualcomm Completes Acquisition of NUVIA
feed.summary: Qualcomm Incorporated announced the completion of its acquisition of NUVIA for $1.4 billion. This acquisition aims to enhance Qualcomm's CPU roadmap, integrating next-generation CPUs across various products including smartphones, laptops, and automotive systems. The first Qualcomm Snapdragon platforms featuring these new internally designed CPUs are expected to sample in the second half of 2022.

feed.title: Applied Materials brings AI and big data into semiconductor inspection machines
feed.summary: Applied Materials has introduced new optical semiconductor wafer inspection machines that integrate big data and AI to enhance defect detection and accelerate chip manufacturing. These systems, including Enlight and ExtractAI technology, aim to address the increasing complexity and cost of chip production by improving inspection efficiency and reducing yield loss. The AI-driven approach helps chipmakers identify critical defects faster and adapt to changing manufacturing processes, ultimately boosting profitability.

feed.title: Boston Limited Now Offering AMD EPYC™ 7003 Series Processor Powered Systems
feed.summary: Boston Limited has announced the availability of high-performance systems featuring the new AMD EPYC™ 7003 Series Processors. These processors, based on the "Zen 3" microarchitecture, offer significant performance improvements and are designed to speed up data analytics and deliver faster time to value for businesses. Boston's solutions, including the Quattro Series, aim to meet the demands of industries like media and entertainment, manufacturing, design, and engineering, by providing high processing performance within customers' power budgets.

feed.title: AMD adds seven-nanometer Ryzen Pro 5000 processors for business laptops
feed.summary: AMD has introduced its new Ryzen Pro 5000 series processors for business laptops, following the launch of new server chips. These seven-nanometer Zen 3 core processors include the top-end 5850U, which offers significant performance improvements and extended battery life compared to Intel's offerings. The Ryzen Pro 5000 series also incorporates advanced security and management features, such as shadow stack technology and Memory Guard, to protect corporate data and facilitate remote IT management.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries Eyes Investing Millions To Expand Operations At Its Chip Plant In Malta
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries is planning to invest millions of dollars to expand operations at its chip plant in Malta, New York, as part of a larger $1.4 billion investment across three sites globally. The expansion is driven by growing chip demand and is contingent on federal and state subsidies, particularly from the U.S. CHIPS for America Act. The company also recently secured ITAR certification for U.S. Department of Defense production, indicating its strategic importance as a domestic chip manufacturer.

feed.title: AMD Strikes Back At Intel vPro With Ryzen Pro 5000 CPUs
feed.summary: AMD has launched its new Ryzen Pro 5000 processors for business laptops, directly targeting Intel's vPro lineup. AMD claims these new CPUs offer superior multi-threaded performance, energy efficiency, and enterprise-grade security features that can compete "toe-to-toe" with Intel's offerings. The company expects a significant increase in commercial notebook design wins with these new chips.

feed.title: Laptop Bench Test: Dell Latitude 5410 – An upgrade in mediocrity?
feed.summary: This article reviews the Dell Latitude 5410 laptop, evaluating if it offers significant upgrades over its predecessor or remains a mediocre device. The review details specifications, performance benchmarks (Cinebench, GeekBench), and its suitability for corporate use, concluding it's a solid "fleet laptop" for business tasks rather than a high-performance personal device.

feed.title: AMD says it won't try and stop crypto miners from using its graphics cards
feed.summary: AMD has announced it will not restrict cryptocurrency miners from using its graphics cards, a strategy that contrasts sharply with rival Nvidia's efforts to limit GPU processing power for mining. This decision comes as GPU demand from both gamers and miners has led to frequent shortages. Nvidia previously attempted to curb mining on its RTX 3060 GPU but accidentally released a driver that bypassed the restriction.

feed.title: WUGI Stock Price | Esoterica NextG Economy ETF
feed.summary: This article provides current and historical financial data for the Esoterica NextG Economy ETF (WUGI), including its current stock price, trading ranges, dividends, and key statistics like expense ratio and market cap. It also details the ETF's holdings, with major positions in companies like Taiwan Semiconductor, NVIDIA, and Broadcom. The fund's profile highlights its investment strategy across communication services and information technology sectors.

feed.title: Intel to Spend $20 Billion on 2 New Chip Factories in Arizona. (Published 2021)
feed.summary: Intel's new CEO, Patrick Gelsinger, announced plans to invest $20 billion in two new chip factories in Arizona. This move signifies Intel's commitment to doubling down on chip manufacturing in the U.S. and Europe, aiming to become a major manufacturer for other companies, addressing concerns about component shortages and reliance on Asian factories. The initiative also seeks to regain Intel's footing in the competitive chip production market, where it had previously fallen behind competitors like TSMC and Samsung.

feed.title: In $20bn push, Intel hopes to make US chip-making great again
feed.summary: Intel announced a significant expansion of its advanced chip manufacturing capacity with a $20bn investment to build two factories in Arizona, aiming to regain its market leadership and challenge rivals TSMC and Samsung. CEO Pat Gelsinger's strategy includes opening Intel's plants to outside customers in a "foundry" business model and focusing on cutting-edge computing chip manufacturing. This move is also a response to US and European concerns about the concentration of chipmaking in Asia and seeks to boost domestic production.

feed.title: NZXT launches its first AMD motherboard with the new N7 B550
feed.summary: NZXT has launched its first AMD motherboard, the N7 B550, expanding its product catalog to include AMD platforms. This premium motherboard features a 12+2 power phase design, Wi-Fi 6, support for up to 4733MHz RAM, and PCIe 4.0, retailing for $229.99. The N7 B550 is compatible with Ryzen 3000, 4000, and 5000 series processors and incorporates NZXT's signature sleek design with a customizable metal shroud.

feed.title: China EV maker Nio halts production for 5 days on chip shortage
feed.summary: Chinese electric vehicle manufacturer Nio has announced a five-day halt in production due to a global shortage of semiconductor chips. This decision highlights the widespread impact of the chip scarcity on the automotive industry. Nio is one of several EV makers affected by supply chain disruptions.

feed.title: HUT 8 Expands Mining Capacity with $30 Million USD Purchase of NVIDIA CMPs
feed.summary: Hut 8 Mining Corp. has announced a $30 million USD investment in NVIDIA CMPs to significantly boost its mining capabilities, increasing its aggregate operating rate by approximately 1600 Gigahash. This acquisition, with full deployment expected by summer 2021, aims to diversify Hut 8's revenue streams by mining alternative blockchain networks and exploring high-performance computing applications, while still receiving payouts in Bitcoin. The move strengthens Hut 8's strategy for immediate and long-term revenue growth.

feed.title: The China challenge: Why Intel is too strategic to fail
feed.summary: This article analyzes Intel's strategic shift to IDM 2.0 under CEO Pat Gelsinger, focusing on the company's plan to leverage internal manufacturing, external foundries, and a new Intel Foundry Services division. It examines the market changes impacting Intel, the critical threat from China's semiconductor ambitions, and the need for significant partnerships, including with IBM and the U.S. government, for Intel to regain its leadership and combat the China challenge. The authors argue that despite positive steps, Intel needs even more substantial changes, possibly a joint venture with a volume leader like Samsung, to succeed.

feed.title: Miner Hut 8 Purchases $30 Million of Nvidia CMPs
feed.summary: Canadian Bitcoin miner Hut 8 has purchased $30 million worth of Nvidia CMPs (GPUs) to expand its mining capabilities beyond just Bitcoin. This move allows for versatility in mining various cryptocurrencies, participation in staking and DeFi, and potential repurposing for High-Performance Computing. The purchase reflects a broader trend among miners to diversify their operations and holdings in the current bull run.

feed.title: Technology Industry Veteran, Geoffrey Ribar, Named SambaNova Systems Interim Chief Financial Officer
feed.summary: SambaNova Systems announced the appointment of technology industry veteran Geoffrey Ribar as interim Chief Financial Officer. Ribar brings extensive financial and leadership experience from various high-tech companies, including Cadence Design Systems and NVIDIA, which will be crucial for SambaNova's strategic expansion in the AI and data-intensive applications market. His appointment is expected to strengthen the company's financial initiatives and market leadership.
